lines.en_lit: like one who lashes with a whip taught When sprinkles he does not sprinkle on the Ark cover; rather, against the thickness of the Ark cover. When he sprinkles upward, he turns his hand downward, And when he sprinkles downward, he turns his hand upward.

From where are these matters, Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said Rabbi Zeira said: The verse states and sprinkle it upon the Ark cover and before the Ark cover” not say downward, to the goat, as is unnecessary; is derived from downward with bull.
why is stated to juxtapose “upon to “before just as “before” that not on so too, “upon” that not on

On the contrary, not say upward, to the bull, as is unnecessary; is derived from upward to goat. why does it state to juxtapose “before to “upon just as “upon” actually upon, so too, “before” actually upon

What is this Granted, if you say downward to the goat to juxtapose it to upward to the bull, one needs for that which the school of Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov taught. As the school of Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov taught: “Upon [al penei] the Ark cover to the east” this forms an analogy Any place where it is stated “face [penei],” nothing other eastern However, if you say, upward to the bull to juxtapose for what purpose does downward to the goat come;
The Sages taught: “And sprinkle it upon the Ark cover and before the Ark cover” We learned how many upward for the goat, one downward for the goat, I do not know how many
I derive It states blood downward of the bull, and it states blood downward of the goat. Just as downward of the bull seven so too, downward of the goat seven

Or go this way: It states blood upward of the goat, and it states blood downward of the goat. Just as upward of the goat one so too, downward of the goat one Let us see what resembles: One derives downward downward, and one does not derive downward from an upward

On the contrary, one derives a matter from that matter, and one does not derive that matter from matter. the verse states: “And he shall do with its blood as he did with the blood of the bull” As no the verse to state: “As he did,” what the verse states: “As he did”? that all his actions should be alike, just as downward of the bull seven, so too, downward of the goat seven.

We have learned how many downward of the bull and by the goat: Seven. I do not know how many upward of the bull. And I derive It states blood upward of the goat, and it states blood upward of the bull. Just as upward of the goat one so too, of the bull upward one

Or go this way: It is stated blood downward of the bull, and it is stated blood upward of the bull. Just as downward of the bull seven so too, upward of the bull seven Let us see what resembles: One derives upward upward, and one does not derive upward from a downward

On the contrary, one derives a matter from that matter, and one does not derive that matter from matter. the verse states: “And he shall do with its blood as he did.” As no the verse to state: “As he did,” what the verse states: “As he did”? that all his actions should be alike: Just as downward of the bull seven, so too, downward of the goat seven; and just as upward of the goat one, so too, upward of the bull one.

One; one and one; one and two. The Sages taught One; one and one; one and two; one and three; one and four; one and five; one and six; one and seven. the statement of Rabbi Meir. Rabbi Yehuda says One; one and one; two and one; three and one; four and one; five and one; six and one; seven and one.
They do not disagree Sage in accordance with in his place, and Sage in accordance with in his place.
In any case, everyone, the first sprinkling must be counted with each and every one What is the reason Rabbi Elazar said: that he does not err in the sprinklings.

Rabbi Yoḥanan said the verse states: and before the Ark cover he shall sprinkle” As no the verse to state: He shall sprinkle” what the verse states: “He shall sprinkle”? teaches to the first sprinkling that it must be counted with each and every one,
What is between between them where he did not count and did not err.
emerged and placed on the golden pedestal in the Sanctuary, We learned there Rabbi Yehuda says: There were no horns for obligatory nests, due to the mixtures.

What is Due to a mixture? Rav Yosef said: due to mixture of obligatory with free-will Abaye said to him: And let us prepare two and write on them which is for obligatory and which is free-will Rabbi Yehuda
